---
title: "Training Report"
author: "Jan Cap"
date: now
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]
#| label: parameters-setup

input_path: str = "data/final/feature_matrix.csv"
training_config_path: str = "configs/training.yaml"

```

```{python}
#| label: libraries-setup

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import shap
import yaml
from IPython.display import display, Markdown
from geoscore_de.modelling.plots.diagnostic_plots import build_predicted_vs_actual_plot, build_residual_plot
from geoscore_de.modelling.plots.grid_search_plots import build_grid_search_results_plot
from geoscore_de.modelling.config import TrainingConfig
from geoscore_de.modelling.train import Trainer
```

```{python}
#| label: load-data

# load input data
df = pd.read_csv(input_path)

# load training config
with open(training_config_path, "r") as f:
    training_config = TrainingConfig(**yaml.safe_load(f))
```

## Input Data

```{python}
#| label: setup-and-data
#| output: asis

# Print summary of feature selection for report
feature_cfg = training_config.feature_filtering

used_features = feature_cfg.use_features or "ALL"
omitted_features = feature_cfg.omit_features or "NONE"

# data shape
print(f"- Total samples: {df.shape[0]}")
print(f"- Total features before selection: {df.shape[1]}")
# train setup
print(f"- target variable: {training_config.target_variable}\n")


print("### Feature selection criteria")
print("\n**Features to use:**", used_features)
print("\n**Features to omit:**", omitted_features)
```


## Model training

```{python}
#| label: model-training
#| output: false

# train model
result = Trainer(training_config).train(df)
```

```{python}
#| label: model-training-desc
#| output: asis

print(f"- Number of train/val samples: {result.X_train_val.shape[0]}")
print(f"- Number of test samples: {result.X_test.shape[0]}")
```

## Best parameters and CV results

```{python}
#| label: best-params-and-cv-results
#| output: asis

cv_results_df = pd.DataFrame(result.grid_search.cv_results_)
best_idx = result.grid_search.best_index_
best_row = cv_results_df.loc[best_idx]
cv_folds = int(result.grid_search.cv)

best_params_df = (
    pd.DataFrame(result.best_params_.items(), columns=["Parameter", "Value"])
    .sort_values("Parameter")
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)

display(Markdown("### Best hyperparameters"))
display(best_params_df.style.hide(axis="index"))
# show res for best params
display(best_row[["params", "mean_test_r2", "mean_train_r2", "std_test_r2", "std_train_r2"]].to_frame(name="Best CV Result"))

# add detail that plots build_grid_search_results_plot
print("<details>")
display(cv_results_df[["params","mean_test_r2", "mean_train_r2", "std_test_r2", "std_train_r2"]].sort_values("mean_test_r2", ascending=False))
display(build_grid_search_results_plot(cv_results_df, result.grid_search.best_params_))
print("</details>")
```

## Model evaluation

```{python}
#| label: train-evaluation

metric_specs = [
    {"key": "r2", "label": "R²", "lower_is_better": False},
    {"key": "mae", "label": "MAE", "lower_is_better": True},
    {"key": "mse", "label": "MSE", "lower_is_better": True},
    {"key": "rmse", "label": "RMSE", "lower_is_better": True},
    {"key": "mape", "label": "MAPE", "lower_is_better": True},
    {"key": "median_ae", "label": "Median AE", "lower_is_better": True},
    {"key": "max_error", "label": "Max Error", "lower_is_better": True},
    {"key": "explained_variance", "label": "Explained Variance", "lower_is_better": False},
]

train_metrics = result.evaluate_subset(result.X_train_val, result.y_train_val, metric_prefix="train")
test_metrics = result.evaluate(create_plots=False)

combined_metrics = []
for spec in metric_specs:
    metric_key = spec["key"]
    train_key = f"train_{metric_key if metric_key != 'r2' else 'r2_score'}"
    test_key = f"test_{metric_key if metric_key != 'r2' else 'r2_score'}"

    if train_key not in train_metrics or test_key not in test_metrics:
        continue

    combined_metrics.append(
        {
            "Metric": spec["label"],
            "Train": train_metrics[train_key],
            "Test": test_metrics[test_key],
        }
    )

metrics_df = pd.DataFrame(combined_metrics)

display(Markdown("### Final refit model: train vs test results"))
display(
    metrics_df.style.format(
        {
            "Train": "{:.4f}",
            "Test": "{:.4f}",
        }
    ).set_caption("Metrics for the refit best model on the train/validation split and untouched test split")
)

# Generate and display diagnostic plots for the refit best model
try:
    diag_plot_path = "model_diagnostics.png"
    display(Markdown("### Model Diagnostics Plot"))
    diag_plot = result.plot_diagnostics(save_path=diag_plot_path)
    display(diag_plot)
except AttributeError:
    pass
```

## SHAP analysis

```{python}
#| label: shap-analysis
#| output: false

# SHAP analysis: Identify most relevant features for model predictions


# Use the best trained model from the earlier section
explainer = shap.Explainer(result.best_estimator, result.X_test)
shap_values = explainer(result.X_test)
```

### SHAP Feature Importance

```{python}
#| label: shap-analysis-fig

# Plot feature importance (summary plot) inline (do not save to disk)
shap.summary_plot(shap_values, result.X_test, show=True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
plt.close()
```

---

### Evaluations per federal state

In this section we break down the model's performance by federal state, based on the municipality identifiers in the test set. 

```{python}
#| label: per-state-table
#| output: asis

STATE_NAMES = {
    "01": "Schleswig-Holstein",
    "02": "Hamburg",
    "03": "Niedersachsen",
    "04": "Bremen",
    "05": "Nordrhein-Westfalen",
    "06": "Hessen",
    "07": "Rheinland-Pfalz",
    "08": "Baden-Wuerttemberg",
    "09": "Bayern",
    "10": "Saarland",
    "11": "Berlin",
    "12": "Brandenburg",
    "13": "Mecklenburg-Vorpommern",
    "14": "Sachsen",
    "15": "Sachsen-Anhalt",
    "16": "Thueringen",
}

test_meta = df.loc[result.test_indices, [training_config.id_column]].copy()
test_meta[training_config.id_column] = test_meta[training_config.id_column].astype(str).str.zfill(8)
test_meta["state_code"] = test_meta[training_config.id_column].str.slice(0, 2)
test_meta["state_name"] = test_meta["state_code"].map(STATE_NAMES).fillna("Unknown")

state_counts = (
    test_meta.groupby(["state_code", "state_name"])
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="Municipalities in test set")
    .sort_values("state_code")
)

state_eval_data = []
for _, row in state_counts.iterrows():
    state_code = row["state_code"]
    state_name = row["state_name"]
    state_mask = test_meta["state_code"] == state_code
    X_state = result.X_test.loc[state_mask.values]
    y_state = result.y_test.loc[state_mask.values]
    metrics_state = result.evaluate_subset(X_state, y_state, metric_prefix=f"state_{state_code}")

    state_eval_data.append(
        {
            "state_code": state_code,
            "state_name": state_name,
            "Municipalities in test set": row["Municipalities in test set"],
            "metrics": metrics_state,
            "X_state": X_state,
            "y_state": y_state,
        }
    )

state_rows = []
for item in state_eval_data:
    state_code = item["state_code"]
    metrics_state = item["metrics"]
    state_rows.append(
        {
            "State Code": state_code,
            "State": item["state_name"],
            "Municipalities in test set": item["Municipalities in test set"],
            "R2": metrics_state[f"state_{state_code}_r2_score"],
        }
    )

state_summary_df = pd.DataFrame(state_rows).sort_values("State Code")
display(
    state_summary_df.style.hide(axis="index").format(
        {
            "R2": "{:.4f}",
        }
    )
)
```

```{python}
#| label: per-state-evaluation
#| output: asis

print("::: {.panel-tabset}")

for item in state_eval_data:
    state_code = item["state_code"]
    state_name = item["state_name"]
    X_state = item["X_state"]
    y_state = item["y_state"]

    print(f"\n### {state_code} - {state_name}")

    y_pred_state = result.best_estimator.predict(X_state)
    diag_plot = build_predicted_vs_actual_plot(y_state, y_pred_state) | build_residual_plot(y_state, y_pred_state)
    display(diag_plot)

    try:
        state_explainer = shap.Explainer(result.best_estimator, X_state)
        state_shap_values = state_explainer(X_state)
        shap.summary_plot(state_shap_values, X_state, show=True)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.show()
        plt.close()
    except Exception as exc:
        print(f"_Could not compute SHAP for this state: {exc}_")

print(":::")
```
